function.name: emit_narrative_plan
function.description: Return the complete narrative architecture for a WebGAL visual novel.

title.description: The title derived from the source material.

theme.description: The core theme of the source material, stated as a concise dramatic idea.

emotion_tone.description: The dominant emotional tone, such as restrained melancholy, romantic tension, absurd satire, or hopeful recovery.

conflict_structure.description: The central conflict pattern, including external pressure, inner contradiction, and relationship tension.

story_progression.description: A phase-by-phase outline of how the adaptation should unfold. Do not write script text here.

name.description: Short label for this story phase.

content.description: What should happen in this phase, including emotional movement and player choice opportunities.

story_arc.description: The overall emotional and dramatic arc from opening state to possible resolution.

characters.description: Major characters needed for the adaptation.

personality.description: Personality with contradictions, not a flat archetype.

motivation.description: What the character wants and why it matters emotionally.

speech_style.description: How the character speaks, with rhythm, vocabulary, and social texture.

emotional_arc.description: How this character may change or fail to change across the story.

dynamic.description: The dramatic relationship dynamic and how it creates tension.

touchable_points.description: Emotionally touchable moments the player should feel, such as misunderstanding, tenderness, shame, hesitation, or estrangement.

social_observation.description: Social or psychological observations extracted from the material.

function.name: emit_asset_manifest
function.description: Return the complete generated-image manifest derived from the narrative plan.

function.name: emit_scene_batch
function.description: Return validated WebGAL scene file contents as data, not direct file writes.

beats.description: Short narrative/dialogue beats. Backend renders them into WebGAL syntax.

function.name: emit_validation_report
function.description: Return a structured validation report for a generated WebGAL game.

function.name: emit_repair_plan
function.description: Return targeted scene-file repairs for validation errors.
